---
title: "GitHub"
toc: true
toc-location: right
---

**What you need to do:**

- Create a GitHub account using your **personal** email (not WB email)
- Join the World Bank GitHub organization via eServices
- Request GitHub Copilot access from the WB organization or subscribe privately

---

## Access Levels

Different setup steps unlock different capabilities:

| Setup | Repositories | GitHub Copilot | WB Org Repos |
|-------|-------------|----------------|--------------|
| No GitHub account | Public only | ❌ | ❌ |
| Personal account | Personal repos | ⚠️ Limited, no Claude | ❌ |
| + Copilot Pro/Pro+ subscription | Personal repos | ✅ Full (500/1500 req/mo), Claude | ❌ |
| + WB Org member | Personal + WB repos | ⚠️ Basic only | ✅ |
| + WB Org + WB Copilot access | Personal + WB repos | ✅ Full (300 req/mo), Claude, GPT-4.5 | ✅ |

::: callout-tip
For AI-assisted coding you need at minimum a personal GitHub account with Copilot access (private subscription **or** WB Copilot access).
:::

---

## Step 1: Create a GitHub Account

Create an account at <https://github.com/signup>.

::: callout-warning
Use your **personal email address**, not your World Bank email.
:::

---

## Step 2: Join the World Bank GitHub Organization

1. Submit a [Join GitHub Organization Account Request](https://worldbankgroup.service-now.com/wbg/en/join-github-organization-account-request?id=wbg_sc_catalog&sys_id=910e1739db1a54903c5960ab13961912&table=sc_cat_item&searchTerm=github) on eServices to join [github.com/worldbank](https://github.com/worldbank)
2. Accept the invitation email sent to your GitHub account email
3. Confirm membership by following the steps in the email

::: callout-note
If you don't accept the invitation within a few days it expires — resubmit through eServices.
:::

::: callout-warning
**Inactivity policy.** Members who have not contributed to any WB GitHub repository for several months may be removed from the organisation automatically. If you lose access, resubmit the eServices request to rejoin. This does **not** affect your personal GitHub account or Copilot subscription.

<!-- TODO: confirm exact inactivity period with github@worldbank.org and update this note -->
:::

---

## Step 3: Request GitHub Copilot Access

After joining the WB org, go to [GitHub → Settings → Copilot](https://github.com/settings/copilot).

![Copilot settings page](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/images/gh-copilot-access-1.png)

Scroll down to **"Get Copilot from an organization"** and click **Request access** from the worldbank organization.

![Request access button](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/images/gh-copilot-access-2.png)

Then send an email to [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org) with:

- Subject: `Request GitHub Copilot Access`
- Body: your GitHub username + brief reason
- Copy your manager for approval

::: callout-tip
[Bryan Cahill](mailto:bcahill@worldbankgroup.org) and [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org) manage the WB org and can help.
:::

---

## Step 4: Verify Copilot Access

Go to [GitHub → Settings → Copilot](https://github.com/settings/copilot) and check your usage statistics.

**No access:**

![No Copilot access](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/images/gh-copilot-access-3a.png)

**Access granted:**

![Copilot access confirmed](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/images/gh-copilot-access-4a.png)

---

## Step 5: Overuse Budget (Optional)

The WB plan includes 300 premium requests/month. To enable budget for overuse:

1. Get manager approval for a budget code
2. Set a monthly spending limit
3. Email [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org) with your budget code (cc your manager)

<!-- TODO: add direct link to the WB internal page / eServices form for Copilot overuse budget request -->
